item.title: Anti-Amyloid Alzheimer's Drugs Debate
item.description:
• Summary: A Cochrane review questions the efficacy of anti-amyloid Alzheimer's drugs like lecanemab and aducanumab, describing their effects as "absent or trivial." This reignites the debate over the amyloid hypothesis, with immediate backlash from proponents and industry stakeholders, highlighting ongoing controversies in Alzheimer's treatment.
  • MoAs: Monoclonal antibodies targeting amyloid-beta plaques to reduce neurotoxicity in Alzheimer's disease.
  • Sources: Fierce Pharma, Endpoints News, STAT News
